How do you interview and how do you actually hire people? I've talked about the interview process in the IQ, EQ, and Abstitute Test section, but I'm going to break it down for you guys. Once again, I go through a two to three phase interview process, depending on who it is. Phase number one, we do a video interview or sometimes an in-person meeting depending on if the person is in Vegas or Miami. That being said, if it's not in Vegas or Miami, we're doing a video interview. When we get on the phone with them and we're talking with them, we're going to give you guys some of the interview questions and some of the previous videos. Here's some good questions that you can ask maybe on a lower level of starting your digital agency or some of the questions that I ask inside of my organization. Once again, the great question that I'd like to end it off with are what are two bad habits that you still have today? What makes you a good team member? Why do you want to work for my organization? What intentions do you have with working for my organization? What is it that you have as personal goals that I can help you fulfill? Do you have any short-term goals or long-term goals that you want to explain to us? Is there any room for you to grow into some kind of manager? Are you looking for a promotion long-term or short-term? Have you had any experience doing what we need you to do in our organization with what you're going to be doing now or what you've done historically? Do you do anything on the side? How much time can you dedicate to us? How did you find us? Have you heard anything about us? All these questions that you ask give you a perspective on the individual and how they may think and how they may be operating. Not a lot of questions in the interview are really my experience in history. Worth anything because most of the questions that you ask, unless they're trick questions that kind of open up their psychology like some of the things I just mentioned, are really going to be good. All these people are going to give you posturing questions that are just fluff because it's an interview. You don't really get to see the person until they go into action. Interview number two is typically done with an IQ and EQ and an aptitude test. I'll put it in the footer of this video here. And like I said before, and I'll say it again in the last course just in case you didn't watch it or you forgot about it. I think it's 60 bucks. I pay $60 per IQ, IQ and aptitude test. And keep in mind, pro tip, don't call the aptitude test an aptitude test. Call it an employee match test or an employee assessment. You want to see if your answer is match up with the personality type of the business. You want to see if it's an employee match test or an employee identifier test or an employee assessment, not an aptitude test. I think that's illegal. My business consultant gave me that advice so I passed that on to you. That being said, after you get past that second interview, you want to roll right into interview number three. As I mentioned in one of the previous courses, you want to have week one and week two of the business or just week one if you do a great job to accomplish whatever it is that you're going to pay them for, to a greater rate to accomplish whatever it is that you're going to pay them for, to a greater rate in the future at the exact same rate that you expect them to perform. So you want to say, hey, listen, I expect you at this $500 test rate to perform as if I was paying you the full rate that I would be negotiating yet. That being said, I want you to complete all the tasks that I need you to and literally crush it. I want you to make sure that you work with my team fluently and appropriately and that you ask all questions and any questions in order to succeed your role. That being said, you want to help the person by establishing one communication line for them in the business, meaning they need one person that they can talk to besides you, going to introduce them to somebody else or if it's just you and them, get them into your business and make sure that they have a clear open line of communication directly to you. That's helping them overcome non-existence, which is one of the conditions we spoke about. That being said, after you brought the person in week number one and they've successfully passed your test of just making sure that they are the quality standard that you need and they can't actually accomplish what they said that they've set out to do and you overall assess their behaviors and actions, understand that their overall abilities through time are going to go slightly down unless they're a great employee because then they go slightly up through time. Some people get bought out of the game as time goes on, meaning they lose interest, they lose ability and they lose skill because they're not evolving. For example, my digital marketer blows everybody in my company out of the water with his ability to self-educate because of the fact that he is going out of his way three hours a day to make sure that he's looking at new material, new books, new courses, new information. I think he's actually watching me record this course literally right now for the last four days because that's what he does to self-educate and go up. Whereas I see some of my staff and employees never do anything to self-educate themselves. I get them and watch them get more bought out of the game through time, getting to a point where I have to end up washing them out and firing and bringing in somebody else who's back up here or somebody who has that personality type. You will get mixed people throughout it, but you'll notice that they both start at either the same point or at different points, meaning they'll either get better or they'll get worse. Wherever they start at on this point, you'll never actually see if they're going to get better or worse unless you can prove that they self-educate in the interview process until they actually have a little bit of time in your organization. That's when you start to see habits and actual actions show up. Anyway, this has been how to interview and how to hire people as you actually bring them in to get them into your business. After you've done that first week test once again, all you need to do, get them with an employee agreement, get them with a general, just, hey, this is your job, this is what you're going to execute for us. Have them sign an NDA. I put an NDA for you guys below so you guys can use it in your business. That way everything you say and everything your client say to them legally is under a non-disclosure agreement so they don't have the ability to go out and have an open mouth about all your operations and things like that. Also, depending on who they are and why they're in your organization, you might want to have them sign a non-compete. A non-compete means, and I've actually aired out one of my employees using this and I'll tell you a great story, that salesperson that I had who wanted 30% equity in my organization. She also was somebody who I aired out and got out of my business by putting a non-compete in front of her. I knew her little slimy ass was somebody who actually wanted to come into my business and take the overall principles and things that I was selling and eventually sell them and create it herself. I could sense it in my bones because I have a great way to see the snakes in the grass. What I do is I put a little poison in the air and I air the snakes out to make sure that they show up and then with a rifle on the wrong side, I'm like, shoot them. I make sure that the snake is gone. I piece out, I get them out, I get them out of my organization. So anyway, here's how I did it. This girl, I just sensed it in my bones, in my clums, that she was somebody who was going to just hurt me. She was bad, you know, he felt it. So here's what I did. I went to her and I said, you know, I noticed that with one of our clients, you had mentioned that you actually owned material that you were consulting the business on. And I want to make it very clear that you don't own Diddly Jack shit for what you're performing for our clients. The company is represented. It's not individualized. It's our company that is doing this for this client. It's not you owning anything because you're represented under the company. You can either take this opportunity to go out and be on your own and do everything yourself or you can admit that you're in the company. However, if you want to continue your employment with me, this is where I laid down the non-compete. Great tool. However, if you want to stay in my organization, you're going to sign this non-compete. Here's what I did. I made the non-compete literally to the point where it was so unbelievably strict that I did it purposely to get the fuck out of my business. Like I said, you don't want snakes in your organization whether they sign the non-compete or not. So I went to her and I said, hey, here's a non-compete legally just so you know. A non-compete can only be up for 18 months. I think it can be up to 100 miles of whatever area of business you operate in. I said that she couldn't compete in the entire United States for up to three full years of anywhere that I operated in for business and any country that I operated in for business. I also knew that she came from a legal background so she took it to some of her lawyer friends. She calls me up when I'm on a connecting flight going back to Miami and I was in Fort Worth and she was like, I can't believe you're doing this. Oh my god, this is the worst non-compete ever. There's no way I'm signing this. I was like, looks like you're not going to be a part of my organization. Shucks. That was that. Peace gone. That's exactly how I wanted to get her out. I wanted her to fire herself by not, not, not a, um, sort of a, we're starting to see not a, I fucking hit wall. What's the C word? What's the C word? Uh, comply. There it is. She didn't comply with my order. She didn't comply with what I had requested of her. There's my order. She didn't comply with what I had requested of her. There's nobody once again who laughably, my order. She didn't comply with what I had requested of her. They thought they had started up my business with me when in reality they barely generated more than three leads throughout their entire couple months in my business and barely generated more than one sale. A laughable cause for anybody to even request equity. But more importantly, air that person out with a non-compete. I'll also give you the non-compete below. That's also just to have every single thing that you do shown to me to make sure that they're not going to come into your business if they come from a similar background or if they have an entrepreneurial tendency and want to potentially rip off your business, you're going to show them everything from the internal operations inside out. You're going to literally have every single thing that you do shown to an employee from the inside out. They're a part of the system when they become a staff member. You don't want to have that liability. Sometimes when it's appropriate to have a non-compete, just make sure it's legal. Team Building and Execution Days. I'll see you in the next video. How to interview people inside of phase number seven with Team Building and Execution Days. I'll see you in the next video.
